channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Defence Chief clarifies criteria for rehabilitating ex-insurgents under Nigeria’s deradicalisation programme

item.title: Hegseth urges quieter U.S. defense posture in Asia after Trump China visit amid allied security concerns
item.description: Following a recent visit by former President Trump to China, U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has signaled a shift in tone regarding Washington’s approach to Beijing and broader Indo-Pacific defens...

item.title: Paul McCartney discusses life during Beatlemania and the grounding influence of his family
item.description: Paul McCartney reflected on the intense early years of Beatlemania during a recent appearance on The Zane Lowe Show, where he discussed both his memories of global fame and the personal strategies tha...

item.title: WA AFL legend Bryan Cousins opens up about motor neurone disease diagnosis
item.description: West Australian football legend Bryan Cousins has shared the emotional news that he has been diagnosed with motor neurone disease (MND). Speaking at Swan Districts Football Club in a one-on-one interv...

item.title: Attorney General refers teenage rape sentencing case to Court of Appeal amid public controversy
item.description: The UK Attorney General, Lord Hermer, has stated that he had “no doubt” about referring to the Court of Appeal a controversial sentencing decision involving three teenage boys convicted of raping two ...

item.title: CUET-UG 2026 delayed due to technical glitch; NTA clarifies and assures full exam duration for students
item.description: On 30th May 2026, the Common University Entrance Test-Undergraduate (CUET-UG) faced delays across multiple centres in India due to a technical glitch reported by Tata Consultancy Services (TCS), the N...

item.title: Inside the Enhanced Games: Silicon Valley, Peptides, and the Business of Human Enhancement
item.description: The Enhanced Games, dubbed the 'steroid Olympics,' took place in Las Vegas over Memorial Day weekend, featuring 42 athletes who competed while using performance-enhancing drugs under medical supervisi...

item.title: Hershey Neri discusses survival of assault and her recovery through therapy
item.description: Hershey Neri has shared a deeply personal account of surviving an assault and how she has been coping and rebuilding her life through therapy. In her interview, she opened up about the emotional and p...

item.title: US 30-year mortgage rate rises to 6.53%, reaching nine-month high amid higher bond yields
item.description: The average long-term mortgage rate in the United States continued its upward trend this week, reaching its highest level in nine months and adding further pressure on prospective homebuyers. Accordin...

item.title: Tactical analysis and key factors ahead of PSG vs Arsenal Champions League final in Budapest
item.description: Paris Saint-Germain and Arsenal are set to clash in the UEFA Champions League final in Budapest, with both teams bringing strong narratives into the decisive match. PSG, the defending champions, are a...

item.title: Edmonton experiences severe allergy season due to late spring and dry weather
item.description: Many residents of Edmonton are experiencing a particularly severe allergy season this year, which experts attribute to a late start to spring combined with dry conditions. According to Kevin Sproule, ...

item.title: 16-year-old charged with murder following fatal stabbing of a 15-year-old in Hackney
item.description: A 16-year-old has been charged with the murder of Brayan David Saldarriaga, a 15-year-old boy from Haringey, Hackney, who died after sustaining stab wounds on 25 May. Police were called at 7.17pm to r...

item.title: Man in his 30s hospitalized after shooting in Toronto’s Leslieville neighbourhood
item.description: A man in his 30s was taken to hospital following a shooting incident in Toronto’s Leslieville neighbourhood on the evening of May 29, 2026. Emergency services responded to reports of gunfire in the ar...

item.title: Abia Governor Alex Otti Highlights Infrastructure, Healthcare and Education Achievements After Three Years in Office

item.title: Report Examines Israeli Plans Affecting Bedouin Communities in the Jerusalem Wilderness Area

item.title: Buckingham Palace received archived emails showing Prince Andrew shared confidential information as trade envoy
item.description: Court documents reveal that Buckingham Palace received an archive of approximately 30,000 emails in 2020, which included information showing that Prince Andrew shared confidential government briefings...

item.title: Government Disbands Utumishi Girls Academy Board Following Deadly Dormitory Fire and Safety Violations
item.description: The Kenyan government has dissolved the Board of Management of Utumishi Girls Academy after preliminary investigations into a deadly dormitory fire revealed serious violations of school safety regulat...

item.title: Heavy rain disrupts traffic as Lahore records notable rainfall acrossCity
item.description: Heavy rainfall swept across Lahore, with the heaviest downpour observed in Nishtar Town. Data from the Water and Sanitation Agency (Wasa) show varying rainfall across several neighborhoods, including ...

item.title: Becky Barnicoat Cartoon Highlights the Challenges of Ageing
item.description: The Guardian's latest cartoon by Becky Barnicoat takes a humorous yet poignant look at the realities of growing older. Through her distinctive style, Barnicoat explores the everyday struggles and smal...

item.title: Gabriel DiCecco Convicted of Manslaughter in Friend's Death
item.description: Gabriel DiCecco has been found guilty of manslaughter in the death of his close friend, marking the conclusion of a high-profile trial in Barrie, Ontario. The case revolved around the circumstances th...
